Docs / Getting Started / Quick Start

Quick Start

Get up and running with DevRef in under five minutes. This guide walks you through creating a new project, starting the dev server, and deploying your first site.

Create a new project

Run the following commands to scaffold a new project and start developing:

$ npx create-app@latest my-project
$ cd my-project
$ npm run dev

Project structure

The scaffolded project includes everything you need to get started. Here's what each directory contains:

my-project/
  ├── src/
  │   ├── pages/
  │   ├── components/
  │   └── styles/
  ├── public/
  ├── devref.config.ts
  └── package.json

The src/pages/ directory uses file-based routing. Each file becomes a route automatically. Components go in src/components/ and global styles in src/styles/.

Next steps
Read the Configuration guide to customize your project, or jump straight to Routing to learn how pages work.
